task.objective: Define the shared fault-report data model, an in-memory server-side store, and the Server Actions/read APIs both the customer submission screen and the manager dashboard screen will call: a Fault record (customer_name, car_metadata {brand, model}, description, urgency, safety_advice optional, plus manager-editable fields: affected_systems array, questions_for_customer array, suggested_inspection_items, status enum complete/await_payment/not_started/scheduled defaulting to not_started, scheduled_for) with create/list/get/update operations backed by a module-level in-memory store (no DB, no external persistence).

components.purpose: Turns free-text customer vehicle fault descriptions into structured fault reports for a service adviser/workshop: a customer-facing submission form and a manager dashboard for triaging, prioritizing, and resolving faults.

completion_conditions: A Fault TypeScript type/interface exists covering: customer_name (string), car_metadata ({brand, model}), description (string), urgency (string), safety_advice (optional string), affected_systems (string array), questions_for_customer (string array), suggested_inspection_items (string array), status (enum: not_started/scheduled/complete/await_payment, non-nullable, default not_started), scheduled_for (optional string/date)
A module-level in-memory store (survives across requests within the same server process) holds Fault records keyed by a stable id

Server-side functions/Server Actions exist to: create a fault (customer intake shape only), list all faults, get one fault by id, and update a fault's manager-editable fields (priority/urgency, status, scheduled_for, affected_systems, questions_for_customer, suggested_inspection_items)
Newly created faults default to status = not_started with empty arrays for affected_systems/questions_for_customer/suggested_inspection_items
npm run build succeeds with no type errors
